summary: Bootstrapped noetl/muno as the trip-planner home base and pushed its initial main commit directly to the previously empty repository. The durable deliverables landed: 23 widget payload schemas plus the envelope schema, generated TypeScript contracts, React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + MUI v6 shell, AJV widget validation, JSON stub renderers, docs, scripts, memory skeleton, .claude config, Dockerfile, and nginx config. ai-meta has a local commit adding repos/muno as a submodule. The only AMBER item is container-build verification: local docker is absent and the configured Podman API socket refused connections even after restarting the noetl-dev machine.

frontend_smokes.docker_build_blocker: docker command is not installed; podman build could not connect to the local Podman API socket. podman machine restart completed, but podman still returned connection refused against the configured socket.

widget_renderer_dispatch_smoke.result: Widget dispatch smoke passed for 23 widget envelopes

frontend_skeleton.stubs: src/components/widgets/*.tsx render payload JSON in MUI Cards

cleanup.secret_scan_note: Only placeholder comments and Secret Manager secret names were present; no token bytes, .env files, or node_modules were committed.

verdict: AMBER. The repository bootstrap and all application/schema validations completed and were pushed to noetl/muno main. ai-meta has the local submodule-add commit. The only incomplete verification is container image build due local Docker/Podman connectivity, not project code.
